ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Thai boat noodle soup; closest defensible match is the generic noodle soup food product class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Thai boat noodle soup; A0CKP (noodle soup dishes) is the closest defensible category anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific Thai noodle soup preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: One mini serving of Thai pork boat noodles soup is approximately 300 g, comprising a small bowl of rice noodles, pork meatballs, broth, and vegetables, typical of a hawker-style mini portion.

nutrient_source.title: Mini Thai Pork Boat Noodles (Soup) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Mini Thai Pork Boat Noodles (Soup), cr_id F05060088, confidence 0.95. Saturated fat not reported by HPB; estimated from USDA FoodData Central pork meatball noodle soup analogue (SR Legacy #18240 and #10006 composite) and documented accordingly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — rice noodles analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC, Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Mini Thai Pork Boat Noodles Soup. GI estimated at 55 based on rice noodles (fresh/boiled) GI values of 40–61 reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). A midpoint of 55 is used as the defensible analogous estimate. Per standard GI classification (University of Sydney), GI 55 falls at the upper boundary of the low category (low ≤ 55). gi_category is therefore set to 'low'. GL per serving calculated as: GI × carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 55 × 29.4 / 100 = 16.2 (300 g serving × 9.8 g carbohydrate/100 g = 29.4 g carbohydrate).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content at 493 mg/100 g; a 300 g serving delivers approximately 1,479 mg sodium, representing 64% of the WHO daily limit of 2,300 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should consume this dish sparingly, request reduced fish sauce and soy sauce, and avoid drinking the broth to substantially lower per-meal sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Rice noodles carry a low-to-medium GI (~55) and a 300 g serving delivers approximately 29.4 g carbohydrate with a glycemic load of 16.2; protein at 4.0 g/100 g partially moderates postprandial glucose rise.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should limit noodle portion size, increase the vegetable proportion, avoid adding sugar or sweet condiments, and pair with a protein source to blunt the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium at 493 mg/100 g poses a cardiovascular risk through blood pressure elevation; fat is relatively low at 2.19 g/100 g with estimated saturated fat of 0.75 g/100 g, which is acceptable, but the sodium burden remains the primary concern.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size, request reduced seasoning, avoid consuming the broth, and choose this dish only occasionally as part of a sodium-controlled dietary pattern.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork (red meat) is rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6), which amplifies inflammatory pathways relevant to RA; sodium at 493 mg/100 g worsens tissue swelling.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should limit frequency of consumption, request reduced seasoning, and monitor individual tolerance to chilli (nightshade) which may trigger flares in sensitive individuals.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mini Thai Pork Boat Noodles Soup (ก๋วยเตี๋ยวเรือหมูมินิ) is a Thai-origin noodle soup dish served in a small bowl format popular in Singapore hawker centres and food courts. It consists of fresh rice noodles (kway teow) submerged in a rich, dark pork-based broth seasoned with fish sauce, soy sauce, cinnamon, and aromatic spices. The dish is garnished with mini pork meatballs, bean sprouts, morning glory (water spinach), and Thai basil, with chilli and garlic as condiment options. Per 100 g, it provides 75 kcal, 4.0 g protein, 2.19 g fat, 9.8 g carbohydrate, 2.1 g sugar, 0.6 g dietary fibre, and 493 mg sodium. The low glycemic index (~55, upper boundary of low category) reflects the rice noodle base, and a standard 300 g mini serving delivers a glycemic load of approximately 16.2. Clinically, the dish is notable for its high sodium content, making it unsuitable for hypertension and requiring caution in cardiovascular disease management. For Type 2 Diabetes, the moderate carbohydrate load and low-to-medium GI require portion control and avoidance of sweet condiments. The pork content introduces arachidonic acid, relevant to rheumatoid arthritis management. The dish is dairy-free and suitable for those avoiding lactose, but contains gluten (from soy sauce), soy, and fish allergens. It is a culturally significant Thai street-food preparation adapted into the Singapore hawker context.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05060088, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogous pork noodle soup composite and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database rice noodle analogues with documented basis. All sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (0.75 g/100 g) is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy pork meatball and rice noodle soup analogues, as HPB does not report saturated fat for this entry. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific Thai pork boat noodle soup preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 3. Input cultural tags (primary_cuisine: western, dietary_tags: vegetarian, plant_based, wholegrain) were incorrect and have been corrected: this is a Thai cuisine dish containing pork and is neither vegetarian nor plant-based. 4. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on boiled rice noodles from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this specific dish was found in approved sources. Per University of Sydney classification, GI ≤ 55 is low; gi_category corrected from 'medium' to 'low' to match GI value of 55. 5. Halal status is unconfirmed as the dish contains pork; is_halal set to false.
